entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: Cicer arietinum L., a cultivated legume species

entities.name: C. PINNATIFIDUM
entities.description: Cicer pinnatifidum Jaub, a wild relative of chickpea

entities.name: ANDROGENESIS
entities.description: The process of producing haploid plants from male gametes

entities.name: INTERSPECIFIC HYBRIDIZATION
entities.description: The process of crossing two different species to produce a hybrid

entities.name: HORMONE-AIDED POLLINATIONS
entities.description: Pollination techniques utilizing hormones to facilitate hybridization

entities.name: EMBRYO RESCUE
entities.description: A technique used to save hybrid embryos from aborting

entities.name: TISSUE CULTURE
entities.description: The cultivation of plant tissues in a sterile nutrient medium

entities.name: GRAFTING
entities.description: The process of attaching a shoot to a rootstock for growth support

entities.name: ZEATIN
entities.description: A cytokinin hormone used to induce flowering in hybrid plants

entities.name: CLEISTOGAMY
entities.description: The condition of flowers remaining closed and self-pollinating

entities.name: ACETOCARMINE
entities.description: A staining technique used to visualize cell divisions in anthers

relationships.description: Occurs between Cicer arietinum and C. pinnatifidum
Occurs between Cicer arietinum and C. pinnatifidum
Facilitated by embryo rescue to save aborting embryos
Hybrid shoots were grafted to cultivated chickpea stocks
Induces flowering in the resulting hybrid plants
Hybrid plants produced via this method exhibited cleistogamous flowers
Used to analyze anthers of the resulting hybrid plants
Believed to be the cause of the induction of androgenesis
